mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.5%, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC in >50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real savings indicates robust corporate profitability and healthy consumer balance sheets, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher when both INDPRO and RSAFS rise simultaneously compared to when only one rises, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal improving construction activity and consumer confidence, but this positive effect on equity returns only materializes when inflation is contained below 240.0 on the CPIAUCSL index.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL is below 240.0, or the regime-filtered signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity, which systematically lifts equity valuations over a 5-day horizon through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth and 5-day SPX returns remains negative, or forward returns in payroll growth months are not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%, or the threshold signal produces a positive Rank IC in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real savings and stagnant industrial production indicates a consumption-led growth model that eventually faces supply constraints, predicting negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when RSAFS rises while INDPRO stagnates, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient instead of negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates equity returns over a 5-day horizon only when the unemployment rate remains below 5.0%, as tight labor markets prevent wage-push inflation from eroding profit margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO gains fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns when UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the regime-filtered signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5).
